methodological_ground.detail: AUROC/p-value conflation rules are methodologically sound

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 reliability audit only

reliability.note: Strong unresolved dimension handling; one gap: no guidance for when the only available input is a poster or conference abstract

agent_specific.note: Per-result reliability judgment (different results can get different reliability levels) is a sophisticated and valuable feature

inputs.label: ML prognosis paper with internal validation only — check reliability

assertions.text: Evidence chain behind each claim reconstructed before reliability judgment
Internal validation not equated with generalizability
AUROC not equated with reliability or robustness
Four-level reliability judgment assigned (High/Moderate/Limited/Low) with reason
Different results in same paper given separate reliability levels if warranted

inputs.label: Cohort study with confounding control — assess bias and statistical reliability

assertions.text: Design fit, confounding control, and leakage risk assessed
Sample size vs analysis complexity burden assessed
Statistical significance not equated with reliability
Bottom-line reliability judgment given with traceable reasons
Self-critical risk review present

inputs.label: Omics paper with impressive performance metrics — check if findings are stable

assertions.text: Overfitting and optimistic reporting risks assessed
Validation chain (none/internal/external/orthogonal) distinguished
Conclusion overreach check applied
High performance metrics not praised as reliability by default
Hypothesis-generating label applied when validation is insufficient

inputs.label: Mechanism paper — check if claims exceed what experiments show

assertions.text: Mechanism experiment design identified correctly
Association→mechanism upgrade in claims detected and flagged
Perturbation specificity vs pathway proof distinction applied
In vitro findings not generalized to human clinical claims
Reliability labeled as Limited when evidence chain is mechanistic-only

inputs.label: Multi-result paper with heterogeneous reliability across different claims

assertions.text: Each major result family assessed separately with its own reliability judgment
No single paper-level reliability label forced when heterogeneous reliability exists
Main Result Reliability Map table present with per-claim rows
Hidden fragility not resolvable from report explicitly acknowledged
Citable vs hypothesis-generating verdict given per claim

key_strengths: Per-result reliability judgment allows different levels for different claims within one paper — prevents misleading paper-level averaging
Four-level reliability classification with traceable reason requirement prevents vague 'seems reliable' outputs
Validation chain framework distinguishing six levels (none/internal/external/orthogonal/mechanistic/prospective) is methodologically rigorous
Fifteen hard rules preventing AUROC/p-value/validation conflation cover the most common biomedical result inflation errors

recommendations.title: No guidance for conference poster or abstract-only input
recommendations.problem: The skill handles partial-text inputs but does not specify behavior for conference posters or abstracts-only, which lack methods detail needed for reliability auditing.
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation focuses on paper/abstract/methods combinations but not the poster/conference-abstract format.
recommendations.fix: Add a conference-abstract handling note: when only a conference abstract is available, label the audit as 'Minimal Coverage — methods insufficient for full reliability judgment'. Provide a design-level reliability assessment only and explicitly recommend obtaining the full paper or methods section.

recommendations.title: Description could be more specific about the machine-learning and omics use cases
recommendations.problem: The description adequately covers the general function but does not include trigger phrases specific to the ML and omics paper reliability scenarios that are the highest-demand use cases.
recommendations.root_cause: Description written for general coverage rather than triggering optimization.
recommendations.fix: Add to description: 'Also triggers for machine-learning prognosis papers with internal validation only, omics studies with weak external validation, and mechanistic papers that overclaim causal conclusions from correlation data'.
